task_context.description: This is a comprehensive reverse-engineering and simulation validation task that involves extracting technical specifications from a historical detector design document, implementing a computational physics simulation based on those specifications, and then using the simulation results to propose design improvements. The task requires autonomous document analysis to extract design parameters (not experimental results), creation of a simplified but representative GDML geometry model of the CMS electromagnetic calorimeter, Monte Carlo particle simulation using Geant4, performance analysis of the simulated detector response, and engineering optimization based on the simulation findings. The task specifically emphasizes using only design specifications rather than measured performance data, and focuses on self-contained analysis rather than comparison with published results.
task_context.goal: Extract CMS ECAL design specifications from technical documentation, validate the design through Monte Carlo simulation, and propose improvements to crystal dimensions or segmentation based on simulated performance metrics
constraints: Extract DESIGN specs only, not measured performance values
Use simplified geometry (single module or tower for tractable simulation)
Use homogeneous PbWO4 block or projective tower geometry
Do NOT compare against measured values from the paper
Must test at least one alternative configuration for improvements

observations.content: Step completed successfully: Extract CMS ECAL design specifications from technical documentation including crystal dimensions, material properties, and readout configuration
Step completed successfully: Generate GDML geometry for baseline CMS ECAL design using extracted specifications with projective tower topology
Step completed successfully: Simulate electron showers in baseline CMS ECAL geometry across energy range for performance evaluation
Step completed successfully: Analyze baseline simulation results to compute energy resolution, shower containment, and transverse spread
Step completed successfully: Generate improved geometry configuration with optimized crystal dimensions and enhanced segmentation
Step completed successfully: Simulate electron showers in improved geometry configuration to validate proposed enhancements
Step completed successfully: Analyze improved design simulation results to compute performance metrics
Step completed successfully: Compare performance metrics between baseline and improved CMS ECAL configurations

data.output:
{'report': '# Monte Carlo Simulation Study of CMS Electromagnetic Calorimeter Design Optimization

## Abstract

This study presents a Monte Carlo simulation-based analysis of the CMS electromagnetic calorimeter (ECAL) design using Geant4. Two detector configurations were evaluated using homogeneous PbWO₄ crystal geometry with projective tower topology. Energy resolution and containment were measured for photon energies of 1, 5, and 20 GeV. The analysis reveals concerning anomalies in the sim

methodology_notes: Planned 9-step workflow: extract_cms_ecal_specs, generate_geometry_baseline_design, simulate_baseline_design, analyze_baseline_design, generate_geometry_improved_design, simulate_improved_design, analyze_improved_design, compare_configurations, generate_final_report
